title: Deyeuxia hieronymi
intro: Deyeuxia hieronymi, also known as Hieronymus' Deyeuxia grass, is a type of flowering plant in the grass family (Poaceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities that contribute to its use in treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Deyeuxia hieronymi include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Deyeuxia hieronymi is often used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Deyeuxia hieronymi is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. This classification places Deyeuxia hieronymi among the monocotyledonous plants, characterized by having one cotyledon in their seeds. Further, it is classified within the subfamily Pooideae, a large group of grasses that include many economically important species. The genus Deyeuxia is a small group of grasses native to South America, and Deyeuxia hieronymi is one of the species within this group. More specifically, its classification is within the tribe Poeae, which includes a wide range of grasses found in various parts of the world.
